youtube_items.title: [YT] New energy storage technology on the verge of a breakthrough? | Transforming Business
youtube_items.summary:
All around the world, wind and
solar power plants are multiplying. Clear signs of the energy transition. But for it to succeed,
one key question remains. How do we store electricity? One option is in liquids. So how does that work? Can this technology compete? And why is China ahead? In late December 2024, Germany was
blanketed by thick clouds with barely a breath of wind. A situation known
as the Dark Doldrums. For renewable energy,
it's a huge challenge. Under these conditions, solar and
wind farms produce only a fraction of their usual output. The result is energy shortages
and rising electricity prices. To balance these
fluctuations, large-scale energy storage is essential. And that's where
battery technology comes in. By 2050, more than 1000 gigawatts

youtube_items.title: [YT] What Solar’s 2025 Breakthroughs Mean for 2026
youtube_items.summary: I called 2024 the year of perovskites. We got our first commercial shipments and some record-breaking efficiencies. But 2025? Well, this was the year perovskites went from interesting lab tech to you can actually buy these. Oxford PV shipped the first commercial batch to a US [music] solar farm. California startups landed major infrastructure deals. And Chinese manufacturers started licensing the technology for mass production. More importantly, researchers solved problems that have plagued perovskites for years. We got self-healing materials that repaired damage from heat and moisture, cooling systems that boost efficiency by over 12%, >> [music] >> and manufacturing techniques that broke the 34% efficiency barrier, which shatters silicon's theoretical 30% limit. So, let's recap what actu

youtube_items.title: [YT] How the U.S. Just Handed the Renewable Future to China
youtube_items.summary: This summer, the United States made a $559 billion decision. The consequences of that decision could make your next car $7,500 more expensive and send your electricity bills soaring unpredictably if they aren't already. On July 4th, 2025, President Trump signed the One Big Beautiful Bill Act into law. But this bill that was evidently supposed to make states more self-sufficient really just gave our energy independence away. That's because 3.5 million people in the US work in clean energy jobs, representing 42% of all energy jobs in the country. Meanwhile, coal only employs about 42,000 people. So, solar employs nearly 7 times more US workers than coal. Yet, the current administration is cutting hundreds of billions of dollars from the sector creating jobs while keeping subsidies for the se

analysis.problem: The energy transition is hindered by the lack of efficient energy storage solutions, which poses a challenge to the widespread adoption of renewable energy sources
analysis.root_cause: The current energy storage technologies are not yet cost-competitive with fossil fuels, and the development of new technologies is slow due to lack of investment and policy support
leverage_points: Government policies and regulations that support the development and deployment of energy storage technologies
Investment in research and development of new energy storage technologies

proposed_actions.action: Increase investment in energy storage research and development
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: Achieve a 20% reduction in the cost of energy storage technologies within the next 5 years

proposed_actions.action: Implement policies that support the deployment of energy storage technologies, such as tax incentives and grid connection incentives
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: Increase the deployment of energy storage systems by 50% within the next 3 years

evidence: The lack of energy storage solutions is a key challenge to the energy transition, as stated in the video 'New energy storage technology on the verge of a breakthrough?'

The development of new energy storage technologies, such as perovskites, is showing promise, but more investment is needed to bring them to market, as stated in the video 'What Solar's 2025 Breakthroughs Mean for 2026'
analysis.generalization: The energy transition is connected to other domains, such as the economy and the environment, and the lack of energy storage solutions has implications for the overall sustainability of the system
analysis.summary: The energy transition is hindered by the lack of efficient energy storage solutions, and increased investment and policy support are needed to develop and deploy new technologies. This problem has implications for the overall sustainability of the system and is connected to other domains, such as the economy and the environment.

raw_items.title: New clean energy policy podcast launched by Factor This and Advanced Energy United
raw_items.summary: Factor This is teaming up with the national multi-technology business association Advanced Energy United to launch a new podcast that explores the policy decisions impacting the energy transition.

raw_items.title: How we benefit from transmission competition | Factor This Policycast
raw_items.summary: The first episode of the new Factor This Policycast, an energy policy-focused podcast partnership with Advanced Energy United, focuses on the benefits of competitively bidding large-scale transmission projects.

raw_items.title: Can PJM’s new ‘fast track’ process accelerate interconnection?
raw_items.summary: The Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC) has approved PJM's request to create an Expedited Interconnection Track (EIT), a temporary fast-track interconnection process designed to bring a handful of large "shovel-ready" generation projects online more quickly.

raw_items.title: The largest wind project in the U.S. is almost online
raw_items.summary: The SunZia Wind project in New Mexico, developed by Pattern Energy Group, will generate 3,650 megawatts and export power to Arizona and California.

raw_items.title: Azerbaijan Moves Into Uzbekistan’s Fast-Growing Mining Sector
raw_items.summary: Azerbaijan and Uzbekistan are strengthening economic connections, signing a variety of agreements covering mining, banking and tourism. raw_items.title: Oil Prices Are Crashing. Inventories Tell A Different Story.
raw_items.summary: Oil traders have gone from pricing in the worst supply disruption in modern history to pricing in a recovery that hasn't happened yet. Inventory data suggests they’re getting ahead of themselves. Brent crude fell below $77 per barrel on Thursday, the lowest level since the early days of the Middle East war, after the United States and Iran signed a memorandum of understanding aimed at reopening the Strait of Hormuz and launching 60 days of negotiations toward a broader agreement.
